notes.anchor: unless he has gotten himself aboard ship I think he will be picked off
notes.note: Cicero foresees Pompey's only escape from Caesar's pursuit lies in embarking by sea; he was in fact making for Brundisium to ferry his forces across the Adriatic to Greece, which he accomplished in mid-March 49 BC.

notes.anchor: you will have Terentia with you by the Ides
notes.note: The Ides of February fell on the 13th; Cicero expects his wife Terentia to reach Atticus at Rome by that date.
